evals.name: react-prop-drilling-and-async-states
evals.prompt: Refactor this component to drop the prop drilling and add proper loading/error states. The file is UserDashboard.tsx and package.json shows the project's deps.
evals.expected_output: Detects React from package.json/imports and loads references/react.md. Removes prop drilling (context or restructuring), and adds explicit loading + error (+ ideally empty) states instead of rendering against a possibly-null user. Suggests a query library or at least race-safe effect fetching. Flags the `any` types.

assertions.text: Detects the framework as React (from package.json deps and/or react imports) and loads references/react.md
Removes prop drilling — lifts/shares user via context or restructures, rather than threading `user` through Header/Sidebar/Nav/Content
Adds explicit loading AND error handling instead of rendering against a possibly-null/undefined user
Recommends a server-state/query approach or makes the effect fetch race-safe (cleanup/ignore/abort)
Does NOT introduce styling/Tailwind changes or a test-runner tutorial (out of scope)

evals.name: solid-list-wont-update-in-place
evals.prompt: My SolidJS list won't update when I edit an item in place — what's wrong? See TodoList.tsx; package.json has the deps.
evals.expected_output: Detects SolidJS and loads references/solidjs.md. Identifies the root cause: mutating the store object in place (todo.title = ...) plus setting the same array reference does not trigger fine-grained reactivity — must update through the store setter path (setTodos(i => i.id === id, "title", value)) or use produce. Explains Solid's once-only / fine-grained signal model rather than React's re-render model.

assertions.text: Detects the framework as SolidJS and loads references/solidjs.md (not the React reference)
Identifies the root cause as in-place mutation + reusing the same reference bypassing fine-grained store reactivity
Fixes it via the store setter path (setTodos(...path...)) or produce/reconcile, not a React-style state replacement workaround
Explains the cause in Solid's model (components run once, fine-grained signals/stores) rather than React's re-render/deps model

evals.name: agnostic-hook-typesafety-and-a11y
evals.prompt: Review this hook for type safety and accessibility problems. It's in useToggleSearch.tsx.
evals.expected_output: Type safety: flags `any[]`/`as any` (use generics with constraints, infer types), the unsafe `results[0]` access (noUncheckedIndexedAccess — first may be undefined, selectFirst can crash), and the loosely-typed return tuple. Accessibility: flags the clickable <div> that should be a <button> (not keyboard accessible / no role), missing label association on the input. Stays principle-level / loads React reference since file uses hooks.

assertions.text: Flags the `any[]` / `as any` and recommends generics with constraints or schema-inferred types
Flags the unsafe indexed access (results[0] may be undefined; selectFirst() can crash) tied to noUncheckedIndexedAccess / strictness
Identifies the a11y problem: clickable <div> should be a real <button> (keyboard operable, has role)
Identifies the missing label/association on the search input
Addresses both type safety AND accessibility (does not cover only one)
